name: Resource Optimization Audit
description: Audit resource requests and limits across your cluster to find over-provisioned or under-provisioned workloads.
trendingReason: Right-sizing resources directly reduces cloud costs

steps.title: View actual resource usage vs requests
steps.description: Compare real CPU and memory consumption against what was requested.
steps.tip: If this fails, ensure metrics-server is installed: kubectl get deployment metrics-server -n kube-system.

steps.title: Find pods without resource limits
steps.description: Identify pods that do not have resource limits set, which can cause noisy neighbor problems.
steps.command: kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.containers[].resources.limits == null) | .metadata.namespace + "/" + .metadata.name'

steps.title: View node resource allocation
steps.description: See how much of each node's capacity is allocated to pods.

steps.title: List resource requests and limits for all pods
steps.description: Get a summary of CPU and memory requests/limits across all pods.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Resource Optimization Audit
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Node Resource Usage ==="
kubectl top nodes 2>/dev/null || echo "Metrics server not available"

echo ""
echo "=== Top Memory-Consuming Pods ==="
kubectl top pods --all-namespaces --sort-by=memory 2>/dev/null | head -15 || echo "Metrics server not available"

echo ""
echo "=== Pods Without Resource Limits ==="
kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.containers[].resources.limits == null) | .metadata.namespace + "/" + .metadata.name' 2>/dev/null | head -20

echo ""
echo "=== Node Allocation Summary ==="
kubectl describe nodes | grep -A5 'Allocated resources'

faq.question: What happens if I do not set resource limits?
faq.answer: Pods without limits can consume all available resources on a node, potentially starving other pods. Always set both requests and limits for production workloads.

faq.question: How do I right-size my resource requests?
faq.answer: Monitor actual usage over several days with metrics-server or Prometheus. Set requests to the P95 usage and limits to 150-200% of requests to handle spikes.
